dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 7 responding for unconscious/fainting at --Address Redacted-- in [CITY].
Hello, I'm Nicky, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
She was sitting on the floor in the hotel hallway, unresponsive. We tried to get her to respond, but she didn't move.
Is she breathing?
Yes, she is breathing, but very fast. I think she has a pulse.
Let me check the eye response. Does her pupil show any movement in light?
Pupils are reactive when light is shone. No pinpointing, just normal reaction.
I will check if she moves when her body is touched.
She moves her arm in response to the arm drop test but is otherwise unresponsive.
Any response when we tried to call her name or repeat commands?
No. She didn't respond to any verbal prompts.
She does not respond to painful stimuli applied to her finger.
Her breathing rate appears high-let me count. Approximately 37 to 50 respirations per minute.
Her breathing looks irregular. I see no chest rise-labored or forced breathing.
Pulse is 86, regular.
Respiration rate of 37 per minute.
Blood pressure is 131 over 83.
SpO2 is 0%, despite normal breathing sounds.
I will now perform end-tidal CO2 monitoring to confirm respiratory status. I will place the sensor near her nose.
Capnography shows low readings. Her breathing rate remains elevated.
I'll assess her skin tone and color.
Her skin is pale and cool, with signs of poor perfusion. I suspect pallor from hyperventilation.
Let me test her pupillary response and mental state with light exposure.
Pupils are reactive to light and she elevates her eyes, non pinpoint.
I understand this may be difficult. Can you tell me what happened before the call?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: She had come to see her husband in the hospital. He was just declared brain dead earlier today.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What happened when she found out?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: She became very emotional, hyperventilated, and then collapsed to the floor in the hallway of the hotel.
Any pre-existing medical conditions or history?
She has pulmonary hypertension and hypothyroidism due to Hashimoto's.
What medications is she currently on?
She takes levothyroxine for her thyroid and Hydrochlorothiazide for her hypertension.
Any known drug or food allergies?
No known drug or food allergies.
Does she appear to be experiencing any physical pain at the moment?
I don't think so. She has panic-related symptoms rather than pain.
She is unresponsive to verbal, motor, and pain stimuli. GCS is 7. She is AOX0.
I will now apply the 4-lead EKG for further monitoring of her heart rhythm.
Given her mental status and rapid breathing rate, we will follow the general behavioral disturbance protocol as per protocol 3-4.
I need to educate her on slowing down her breathing. We explained how panic attacks can cause rapid breathing and numbness in the hands.
Vitals improved post-intervention: respiratory rate is now 40 per minute.
Pulse is 78 now, stable.
Blood pressure is 116 over 75.
Oxygen saturation improved to 100% post-intervention.
EKG is now showing normal sinus rhythm.
She remains unresponsive to verbal cues and disoriented.
Glucose was confirmed to be 0 in the second set of results.
Pain remains at 0 throughout the ongoing examination.
Her high breathing rate resolved with proper breathing instructions. She described numbness in her hands afterward.
Based on patient medical history and current condition, we will transport to [COMPANY_NAME] for further evaluation.
Patient is now secured on a stretcher in semi-Fowler's position with seat belt.
During transport, we will closely monitor her vitals to ensure her continued stability.
Upon arrival, she will be sent directly to the emergency department at [COMPANY_NAME] to ED Bed 7.
The patient did not remain conscious after our breathing cues, but her vitals improved in response.
She could not sign due to an altered mental state. EXPECT to use a PUTS form at the hospital.
GCS was 6 with Eyes 1, Verbal 1, and Motor 4 at initial contact.
Her vitals are within the standard range-everything is well-regulated at this point.
She is now breathing at a normal rate and EKG shows normal sinus rhythm.
Assignment cleared. We have followed the general behavioral restraint protocol as per protocol 3-4.
Let me verify the final set of vitals before arrival at [COMPANY_NAME]. Pulse is 78 per minute.
Respiratory rate now at 14 per minute, stable and regular.
Blood pressure remains at 116 over 75 mmHg.
Oxygen saturation levels at 100% and EKG shows normal sinus rhythm.
The patient is in pale and cool condition upon assessment.
All systems go. She is secured and ready to be moved into the ambulance.
Protocol 3-4 upheld throughout. All interventions aligned with this guideline.
